# forecaster.metrics.crps_from_predictions { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.metrics.crps_from_predictions }

```python
forecaster.metrics.crps_from_predictions(y_true, y_pred)
```

Compute the Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS) from predictions.

The CRPS compares the empirical distribution of a set of forecasted values
to a scalar observation. The smaller the CRPS, the better.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                                     | Description                                                                                                     | Default    |
|--------|------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| y_true | [float](`float`)                         | The true value of the random variable.                                                                          | _required_ |
| y_pred | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | The predicted values of the random variable. These are the multiple forecasted values for a single observation. | _required_ |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type             | Description     |
|--------|------------------|-----------------|
|        | [float](`float`) | The CRPS score. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> from spotforecast2.forecaster.metrics import crps_from_predictions
>>> y_true = 5.0
>>> y_pred = np.array([4.5, 5.1, 4.9, 5.3, 4.7])
>>> crps = crps_from_predictions(y_true, y_pred)
>>> crps >= 0
True
```
